mnemonics: N.B. CLASSIFIES WORDS: 'Naive Bayes Calculates Likelihoods, Assuming Features (words) are Statistically Independent, Yet Surprisingly Effective'
keyPoints: It calculates the probability of a word given its class (like a part-of-speech tag) using training data.
It relies on the 'naive' assumption that features (words) are independent of each other given the class.
It is often used as a simple probabilistic baseline for language tasks like text classification or spam filtering.
commonMistakes: Believing the 'naive' assumption of feature independence is true for language (words are highly dependent on context).
Forgetting to apply Laplace (add-one) smoothing, which leads to zero probabilities for unseen words in the test data.
